metadata_checks.comments: Title, DOI, year, paper type, and Sweden carbon-tax/fuel-tax scope all match the paper.

determinant_checks.comment: Key comparison variable; protest membership sharply lowers carbon-tax support across outcomes.
Demographic control; higher education is associated with more carbon-tax support.
Socioeconomic control; income effects are mostly null for carbon-tax attitudes.
Geographic control; rural residence predicts lower support and stronger fairness concerns.
Central substantive mechanism; low trust strongly reduces carbon-tax support.
Political control; left-right orientation predicts carbon-tax acceptability.
Key substantive mechanism; belief in incentive effects raises carbon-tax support.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper. It is a direct carbon-tax acceptability study, and the extraction is tax-specific; the only non-core items are standard demographic/political controls.
